reason: The single residual finding is the absence of `Main` from s1.hints. Since s1 already contains highly specific hints (AsyncMessageSendAction, nablarch.fw.messaging.action.AsyncMessageSendAction, 応答不要電文送信, and all four other related handlers), s1 will be retrieved reliably for any realistic user query about this handler. `Main` is a generic name that adds little discriminative value as a hint; its absence does not prevent the relevant content from reaching the user. No high or medium impact findings remain.

findings_assessment.justification: `Main` is absent from s1.hints but is present in the s1 content (handler queue and related handlers table). s1 carries nine other hints—including the fully-qualified class name and all other related handler names—so any realistic query about AsyncMessageSendAction will retrieve s1 through those hints. A query referencing only 'Main' in isolation is unlikely and would be ambiguous across many handler files. Phase E did not add `Main` to hints, leaving the file unchanged; this was an acceptable judgment given the low discriminative value of such a generic name.
